subject: Chemistry
title: Polonium: Curie's Deadly Legacy
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Polonium: Curie's Deadly Legacy.

hook.text: Marie Curie named polonium after her homeland Poland—then occupied and erased from the map of Europe.

buildup.text: She discovered it in 1898 while searching for the source of extra radioactivity in pitchblende.

discovery.text: Polonium is 250,000 times more radioactive than uranium. A microgram can be lethal.

twist.text: In 2006, Alexander Litvinenko was assassinated with polonium-210 slipped into his tea in London.

climax.text: Named for a country that didn't officially exist. Now known as one of the deadliest substances on Earth.

punchline.text: Named for a lost country. Lethal in a speck. Curie's dangerous gift.

quiz.question: Why did Marie Curie name the element polonium?
options: To honor her homeland Poland, which was occupied at the time
Because it was found near the North Pole
It was named after a Polish king
